---
title: "Exercise 2 — Write Your README with Markdown"
format: html
---

**Module 1 | ~20 minutes**

---

## Goal

Edit your GitHub profile README using Markdown formatting.

---

## Steps

### Open the README editor

1. Go to your repository: `https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/YOUR_USERNAME`
2. Click the **pencil icon** on the README.md file to edit it

### Write your introduction

3. Write at least the following sections using Markdown:

```markdown
# Your Name

## About Me
One or two sentences about your role and research.

## Research Project
Brief description of what you are currently working on.

## Affiliation
- Institution
- Research group / lab

## Education

| Degree | University | Year |
|--------|-----------|------|
| PhD    | UZH       | 2024 |
| MSc    | ...       | ...  |

## Links
- [LinkedIn](https://linkedin.com/in/your-profile)
```

### Commit the file

4. Scroll down to **Commit changes**
5. Write a meaningful commit message: `added introduction and education table`
6. Click **Commit changes**

### Compare

7. Go to `https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME`
8. Compare your profile page to the screenshot from Exercise 1 — what changed?

---

## Check your understanding

**Question:** You wrote `# Your Name` at the top of the file. What would
happen if you used `###` instead of `#`?

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## Click to reveal the answer

It would still render as a heading, but a much smaller one (Heading 3
instead of Heading 1). The number of `#` symbols sets the heading level —
more `#` = smaller text. This matters for document structure: search
engines and screen readers use heading levels to understand a page's
outline, not just its appearance.
:::

**Question:** Your education table used `|` to separate columns. What
happens if a row has fewer `|` separators than the header row?

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## Click to reveal the answer

The table breaks — Markdown table renderers expect every row to have the
same number of columns as the header. A missing `|` usually causes the
remaining cells in that row to merge into the last column, or the whole
table to fail to render as a table at all. Always count your `|` characters
when copy-pasting table rows.
:::

---

## Markdown you practiced

| Syntax | Result |
|--------|--------|
| `# text` | Heading 1 |
| `## text` | Heading 2 |
| `**text**` | **bold** |
| `- item` | bullet list |
| `| col |` | table column |
| `[text](url)` | link |
